attacking_style: Mid-race disruptive attacks designed to fracture the peloton and test sprint teams before final kilometers. Targets moments when sprint trains haven't fully organized, leveraging track endurance to sustain tempo.
team_tactics: Deployed as an early-stage disruptor rather than finale finisher. Used in small breaks (2-man groups) to force rival teams into chase work and create blocking opportunities for teammates.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling stages where his track-developed sustained power can be exploited. Best suited to long-range efforts on courses where pure climbers aren't contesting.
vulnerability: Cannot sustain gaps once organized sprint teams commit full chase trains. Lacks explosiveness to finish off breaks he helps establish when faced with coordinated pursuit.
key_patterns: Deployed in small breaks (2-man) to pressure disorganized pelotons before sprints coalesce (2 instances)
Mid-stage attacks fracture fields, creating tactical blocking opportunities for team strategy (2 instances)
Olympic track endurance credentials used for sustained tempo rather than explosive finishes (2 instances)
Unsuccessful against fully committed, organized sprint team chases in finale (2 instances)
form_trajectory: unknown: insufficient recent data (last observation 2021) to assess current trajectory or development
